title: BAD_SECTOR_SCAN
description: Read-only bad-sector and slow-read test written by m48_bad_sector_scan. Log glob: logs/bad_sector_scan_*.json. IMPORTANT: a sampled scan does not imply a clean bill of health — see coverage_percent, conclusion_strength, and coverage_limitation.

source_module.description: Module that wrote this log (e.g. m48_bad_sector_scan)

schema_version.description: Schema version this log conforms to (e.g. '2.0')

generated.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this report was generated

partition_offset_lba.description: LBA offset of partition start on the physical device (from /sys or parted)

windows_volume.description: Windows volume label or drive letter if determinable (e.g. C:)

filesystem.description: Filesystem type on the scanned partition (e.g. ntfs, fuseblk)

device.description: Block device scanned — legacy alias for partition_device (e.g. /dev/sda2)

scan_scope.description: Defines the exact portion of the device that was (or will be) scanned

device_size_bytes.description: Total size of the scanned block device in bytes

start_lba.description: First LBA included in the scan range

end_lba.description: Last LBA included in the scan range (inclusive)

total_lba_count.description: Total LBAs in the scan range (end_lba - start_lba + 1)

sampled_fraction.description: Fraction of the range actually sampled (0.0–1.0); 1.0 means full scan

full_scan.description: True when every LBA in the range was read (sampled_fraction == 1.0)

scan_mode.description: How sectors were chosen for reading

sampling_strategy.description: Describes how the sample was constructed. 'full' means every sector was read. 'sequential_sample' means evenly-spaced zones. 'targeted' means specific suspicious regions. Used for customer-facing explanation.

coverage_percent.description: Percentage of the partition that was actually read (0–100). For sampled scans this is << 100.

conclusion_strength.description: 'full' = every sector read, conclusion is authoritative. 'sampled' = representative sample, conclusion is probabilistic. 'weak' = very few samples or interrupted. 'unknown' = unable to determine.

tested_ranges.description: LBA ranges that were actually read during this scan

untested_ranges_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was NOT tested. E.g. 'Approximately 97% of disk sectors were not directly read.'

coverage_limitation.description: Standard customer-facing limitation text for sampled scans. E.g. 'A sampled scan checks selected parts of the disk only. No bad sectors were found in the tested areas, but untested areas may still contain bad sectors.'

block_size_bytes.description: Read block size used during the scan

blocks_attempted.description: Total number of read blocks attempted (each block = block_size_bytes)

blocks_read_ok.description: Blocks that returned without read error

blocks_failed.description: Blocks that produced a read error (I/O error, EIO)

blocks_slow.description: Blocks that read successfully but exceeded the slow-read threshold

sector_size_bytes.description: Sector size in bytes (usually 512 or 4096). Duplicate of scan_scope.sector_size_bytes for top-level access.

sectors_covered.description: Total sectors covered by the attempted read blocks (blocks_attempted * block_size_bytes / sector_size_bytes)

bytes_attempted.description: Total bytes of disk read attempted

bytes_read_ok.description: Total bytes successfully read without error

sectors_attempted.description: LEGACY alias for sectors_covered. Kept for backward compatibility.

sectors_read_ok.description: LEGACY alias for blocks_read_ok * sectors_per_block. Kept for backward compatibility.

sectors_failed.description: LEGACY alias for blocks_failed * sectors_per_block. Kept for backward compatibility.

sectors_slow.description: LEGACY alias for blocks_slow * sectors_per_block. Kept for backward compatibility.

slow_read_threshold_ms.description: Milliseconds per block above which a read is classified as slow

bad_ranges.description: Contiguous ranges of blocks/sectors that could not be read

lba_start.description: Logical Block Address of range start

lba_end.description: Logical Block Address of range end (inclusive)

first_seen.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of first error in this range

context.description: File or filesystem metadata near this range if resolvable

slow_read_ranges.description: Contiguous ranges of blocks/sectors with slow (but successful) reads

scan_status.description: Whether the scan finished normally or was interrupted / errored

interrupted_reason.description: If scan_status is interrupted or error, the cause

safety.description: Safety confirmation block — asserts what the scanner did and did not do. REQUIRED. Every field must be verifiable.

read_only.description: True — only read operations were performed; no writes to block device. Always true in v1.

destructive.description: False — no destructive operations performed. Always false in v1.

write_test_performed.description: False — write verification tests were not used. Always false in v1.

stopped_due_to_temperature.description: True if scan was halted because a thermal threshold was exceeded

stopped_due_to_smart_failure.description: True if scan was halted because SMART indicated imminent failure

stopped_due_to_user_limit.description: True if scan stopped because the user-configured time or sector limit was reached

stop_reasons.description: All reasons the scan stopped early, if any (e.g. 'time_limit_reached', 'user_interrupt', 'too_many_errors')

verdict.description: Overall health verdict. For sampled scans use SAMPLE_* prefix to avoid implying a full clean bill of health. OK / HEALTHY-style verdict is only appropriate when full_scan=true and scan_status=completed.

risk_status.description: Normalised risk status using v2 enum. For sampled scans, set to UNKNOWN or the closest match — do NOT set to OK if coverage is < 100%.

confidence.description: Confidence in the verdict. 'high' only when full_scan=true and scan_status=completed. 'medium' for representative sampled scan. 'low' if interrupted or few samples.

errors.description: Runtime errors that occurred during the scan (non-fatal)

smart_correlation.description: Cross-reference with SMART data from disk_health log if available. If SMART shows warnings and the sector scan is sampled-clean, do NOT conclude the disk is healthy.

cross_module_note.description: Human-readable note about agreement or disagreement between sector scan and SMART data

clone_recommended.description: True if bad-sector count, severity, or SMART cross-correlation suggests cloning before further use

ddrescue_command.description: Suggested ddrescue command line for safe cloning if clone_recommended is true

interpretation.description: Report-facing interpretation block. Gives the explanation generator a stable starting point.

customer_summary.description: Plain-English sentence suitable for a customer report

technician_summary.description: More detailed diagnostic note for the technician

what_this_means.description: Explanation of what this result means practically
